chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Allah {swt}, the Almighty, Has Made Permissible and Prohibited in Marriage

text: : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ فَجَرَ بِامْرَأَةٍ ثُمَّ أَرَادَ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ أَنْ يَتَزَوَّجَهَا فَقَالَ "إِذَا تَابَتْ حَلَّتْ لَهُ" قُلْتُ وَ كَيْفَ تُعْرَفُ تَوْبَتُهَا قَالَ "يَدْعُوهَا إِلَى مَا كَانَا عَلَيْهِ مِنَ اَلْحَرَامِ فَإِنِ اِمْتَنَعَتْ فَاسْتَغْفَرَتْ رَبَّهَا عَرَفَ تَوْبَتَهَا ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4457 - Abu al-Maghra narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who committed fornication with a woman and then later wanted to marry her.
Imam (as) said: "If she repents, she becomes lawful for him."
I asked: "And how can her repentance be known?"
Imam (as) replied: "He should invite her to engage in the same unlawful act they previously committed. If she refuses and seeks forgiveness from her Lord (azj), then her repentance is known."
